title: Thelypteris patens
taxonomy: Thelypteris patens is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Thelypteris and is a member of the Thelypteris section of the Pteridophyte plant kingdom. The species Thelypteris patens is further classified under the class Polypodiopsida and division Pteridophyta. This classification is based on its fern-like characteristics, including the presence of true leaves and vascular tissue. Thelypteris patens is native to tropical regions and is often found in shaded areas with high humidity.

intro: Thelypteris patens, also known as the Broad Beech Fern, is a species of fern native to eastern North America. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris patens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoid saponins, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects. Thelypteris patens is often used to make tea, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in herbal medicine to aid in the relief of coughs and colds, as well as to support lung health.
